rar_items.title: Single Leading Underscore (_name)
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: Must explain that a single leading underscore indicates an internal-use/private convention and mention its effect on 'from module import *'.

rar_items.title: Single Trailing Underscore (name_)
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: Must explain that a single trailing underscore is used to avoid conflicts with Python keywords or built-in function names.

rar_items.title: Double Leading Underscore (__name)
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: Must explain 'Name Mangling' within classes and its purpose in preventing name collisions in subclasses.

rar_items.title: Double Leading and Trailing Underscore (__name__)
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: Must explain that these are 'Special/Magic methods' (dunder) reserved by Python and advise against creating custom ones.

rar_items.title: Single Underscore as a Variable (_)
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: Should explain the use of a single underscore as a placeholder for unused values (e.g., in loops or unpacking).

rar_items.title: REPL and i18n Context for Single Underscore
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: May mention the use of '_' for the last expression result in REPL or as an alias for gettext in internationalization.

rar_items.title: Relationship with __all__
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: Should explain how the '__all__' list in a module overrides or complements the single leading underscore rule for exports.

rar_items.title: Clarification on Access Control
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: Should clarify that Python does not have strict private access modifiers and that underscores are primarily conventions (except for mangling).

rar_items.title: Incorrect Access Control Claim
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: Penalize if the response claims that leading underscores provide absolute/strict private access control (like 'private' in Java/C++).

rar_items.title: Confusion between Mangling and Dunder
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: Penalize if the response fails to distinguish between double leading underscores (__name) and double leading/trailing underscores (__name__).

rar_items.title: Target Audience Appropriateness
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The language, tone, and complexity of the explanation must be tailored to a 5th-grade elementary student (approx. 11 years old), avoiding overly academic jargon without explanation.

rar_items.title: Core Achievement Coverage
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must include key achievements such as the establishment of Dosan Seodang/Seowon, the authorship of 'Seonghak Sipdo', and his contributions to Neo-Confucianism (Seongnihak).

rar_items.title: Educational Utility
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide structured summaries, keywords, or presentation tips that directly help the student complete their school assignment.

rar_items.title: Contextual Connection
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should mention relatable facts, such as the 1,000 won bill or UNESCO World Heritage status, to help the student connect the historical figure to the present.

rar_items.title: Moral and Ethical Emphasis
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: Since the request mentions a 'Moral Education' (도덕) textbook, the response should emphasize his virtues, such as integrity (청렴), humility, and self-discipline (수양).

rar_items.title: Structural Clarity
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The information should be organized using bullet points, numbered lists, or clear headings to enhance readability for a young student.

rar_items.title: Supplementary Guidance
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides suggestions for further research, such as recommending specific encyclopedias or official websites.

rar_items.title: Excessive Academic Depth
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response delves too deeply into complex philosophical debates (e.g., detailed metaphysical arguments of the Four-Seven Debate) without simplifying them for a child.

rar_items.title: Inaccurate Historical Facts
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response contains factual errors regarding dates, titles of books, or the relationship between historical figures.

rar_items.title: Meaning Preservation
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The core meaning of the original question (payment completed but 'scheduled report date' still appears) must be preserved in all variations.

rar_items.title: Variation Diversity
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The responses must provide a diverse set of paraphrased questions using different vocabulary (e.g., 이용료, 요금, 비용, 납부, 지불) and sentence structures.

rar_items.title: Conciseness
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: Each variation should be concise and avoid unnecessary wordiness, adhering to the request for brevity.

rar_items.title: Independence of Variations
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: Each generated question must be a standalone inquiry that does not rely on the context of the previous variation.

rar_items.title: Sentence Flexibility
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response should include a mix of complete sentences and fragmented/shortened phrases to reflect natural user behavior.

rar_items.title: Naturalness of Korean
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The paraphrased questions must sound natural and idiomatic to a native Korean speaker in a customer support context.

rar_items.title: Meaning Distortion
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: Any variation that changes the core facts (e.g., saying payment was NOT made, or changing the 'scheduled report date' to a different concept) should be penalized.

rar_items.title: Repetitive Patterns
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: Penalize responses that simply swap one word while keeping the rest of the sentence identical across too many variations.

rar_items.title: Conceptual Correction
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must clearly address and debunk the misconception that a DJ 'just plays music' by redefining the role as a curator, energy designer, or performer.

rar_items.title: Technical Depth of Mixing
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explain core technical tasks such as beatmatching (BPM), phrasing, EQing/filtering, and harmonic mixing (key matching) to justify the skill involved.

rar_items.title: Crowd Reading and Adaptability
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should emphasize the real-time interaction between the DJ and the audience, including reading the floor and adjusting the setlist based on energy levels.

rar_items.title: Preparation and Workflow
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should mention pre-performance work such as track digging, library organization (tagging BPM/Key), and setting cue points/loops.

rar_items.title: Equipment and Tool Knowledge
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should mention the actual tools used (e.g., CDJs, mixers, controllers, software like Rekordbox/Serato) to provide a concrete understanding of the job.

rar_items.title: Diversity of DJ Styles
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides additional value by distinguishing between different types of DJing (e.g., Club, Festival, Turntablism, Hybrid/Live PA).

rar_items.title: Practical Examples of Transitions
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response includes a step-by-step example or a scenario of how a transition actually happens to make the explanation intuitive.

rar_items.title: Over-simplification
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response agrees with the user's premise or fails to explain the 'how' and 'why' behind the tasks, making it sound like a simple playlist playback.

rar_items.title: Excessive Jargon without Explanation
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response uses professional DJ terminology (e.g., 'gain staging', 'phrasing') without providing enough context for a layperson to understand.

rar_items.title: Historical Context Accuracy
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must clearly explain the British colonial history of Hong Kong (approx. 1841/1842 to 1997) as the primary reason for the prevalence of English names.

rar_items.title: Practicality and Communication
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must mention the practical benefits of English names, such as ease of pronunciation and remembrance for non-Chinese speakers, and the complexity of Cantonese romanization.

rar_items.title: Clarification on Universality
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explicitly answer that NOT all Hong Kong residents have English names, correcting the user's potential misconception.

rar_items.title: Celebrity Example Integration
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should address the specific examples provided by the user (Leslie Cheung, Tony Leung, Andy Lau) and explain why English names are particularly common/strategic for public figures (branding, global market).

rar_items.title: Legal vs. Social Distinction
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should distinguish between legal names (usually Chinese) and social/professional English names, mentioning how they appear on IDs or passports.

rar_items.title: Educational and Religious Influence
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response may provide additional depth by mentioning the influence of English-medium schools or Christian baptismal names.

rar_items.title: Comparative Context
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response may provide broader context by comparing the phenomenon with Macau, Singapore, or Mainland China.

rar_items.title: Factual Hallucination
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response must not claim that English names are legally mandatory for all Hong Kong citizens.

rar_items.title: Over-generalization
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response must not imply that all Chinese people in general use English names, failing to specify the unique historical context of Hong Kong.

rar_items.title: Factuality and Nuance
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must acknowledge that an exact, single official number for the total tiles used in Gyeongbokgung is difficult to determine due to multiple construction and restoration periods.

rar_items.title: Contextual Specification
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response should specify the time period (e.g., Heungseon Daewongun's reconstruction in the 1860s) when providing a specific numerical estimate.

rar_items.title: Handling Ambiguity
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should explain why the number varies, mentioning factors such as the original construction, subsequent destructions, and modern restorations.

rar_items.title: Numerical Plausibility
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The provided estimates should be within a reasonable range based on historical records or common academic estimates (e.g., millions of tiles), rather than arbitrary numbers.

rar_items.title: Clarification Request
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response may ask the user for a specific time period to provide a more accurate answer, demonstrating a proactive approach to precision.

rar_items.title: Source Reference
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response mentions specific records or sources (e.g., Gyeongbokgung Yeonggeon Ilgi) to support the provided numbers.

rar_items.title: Irrelevant Humor/Wordplay
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response provides a riddle or wordplay answer (e.g., 'two tiles') as the primary answer, ignoring the factual intent of the user's query.

rar_items.title: Overconfidence in Accuracy
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response presents a single estimated number as an absolute, indisputable fact without mentioning the uncertainty or variance in records.

rar_items.title: Honesty regarding Real-time Data
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must clearly state that a single, real-time, definitive number for the total count of buildings with 5+ floors is not readily available as a static public figure and requires calculation from statistical databases.

rar_items.title: Guidance on Official Data Sources
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must provide specific, actionable paths to official Korean statistical sources, such as KOSIS (Korean Statistical Information Service) or the Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport (MOLIT) building ledger data.

rar_items.title: Definition of 'Floor Count'
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should explain the necessity of defining 'floor count' (e.g., ground floors only vs. including basements or rooftop structures) to ensure the accuracy of the result.

rar_items.title: Clarification of Counting Unit
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should distinguish between the number of 'buildings' (동) and other units (e.g., households or complexes), noting that official statistics typically count by 'building/dong'.

rar_items.title: Methodological Guidance
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should explain how to derive the number from the data (e.g., summing up categories like 5-9 floors, 10-19 floors, etc., or using API filters for groundFloors >= 5).

rar_items.title: Interactive Follow-up Offer
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response should offer to perform the specific search or provide a more detailed guide if the user provides a specific reference year or usage category (e.g., residential only).

rar_items.title: Hallucinated Statistics
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response must not invent a specific, precise number (e.g., 'There are exactly 1,234,567 buildings') without a verifiable, cited source, as this data is dynamic.

rar_items.title: Vague Source Mention
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response should not simply say 'search on the internet' or 'check government websites' without naming specific portals like KOSIS or the Building Ledger API.

rar_items.title: Evolutionary Perspective Alignment
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explain the phenomenon through the lens of evolutionary psychology, focusing on concepts such as survival, reproductive fitness, adaptation, and ancestral environments (e.g., hunter-gatherer contexts).

rar_items.title: Biological Mechanism Accuracy
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must correctly identify the primary hormone involved (Cortisol) and the specific phenomenon (Cortisol Awakening Response, CAR), explaining its role in glucose mobilization and arousal.

rar_items.title: Anticipatory Adaptation Logic
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should explain that the hormone surge is a 'proactive' or 'predictive' preparation for the day's challenges, rather than a mere 'reactive' response to a current stressor.

rar_items.title: Environmental Risk/Opportunity Analysis
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should mention specific ancestral pressures, such as predator avoidance, food foraging, or social competition that occur specifically during the transition from sleep to wakefulness.

rar_items.title: Physiological State Transition
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should describe the transition from the sleep/recovery mode (parasympathetic dominance) to the active/alert mode (sympathetic dominance).

rar_items.title: Evolutionary Mismatch Discussion
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides additional depth by discussing the 'mismatch' between these ancestral adaptations and the modern environment (e.g., artificial light, alarm clocks, chronic stress).

rar_items.title: Circadian Rhythm Integration
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response mentions the role of the Suprachiasmatic Nucleus (SCN) or the interaction between light signals and the HPA axis to explain the timing of the surge.

rar_items.title: Purely Biological Description
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response focuses solely on the biological/medical mechanism (how it happens) without explaining the evolutionary 'why' (the adaptive advantage).

rar_items.title: Misinterpretation of Stress
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response incorrectly frames the morning hormone surge as a negative pathology or a sign of current anxiety/disease rather than a normal adaptive process.

rar_items.title: Definition of Interest Rates
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must clearly define the meaning of interest rates, including the concept of the price of money or the time value of money.

rar_items.title: Yield Curve Concepts
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explain the three primary states of the yield curve: Normal (upward sloping), Flat, and Inverted (downward sloping).

rar_items.title: Bond Price Mechanism
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explain the inverse relationship between interest rates and bond prices, specifically mentioning the impact of duration (maturity) on price sensitivity.

rar_items.title: Equity Valuation Impact
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explain how interest rates act as a discount rate for equity valuation, particularly the higher sensitivity of growth stocks to rate changes.

rar_items.title: Economic Signaling
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should explain the macroeconomic meaning of yield curve inversion as a leading indicator of economic recession.

rar_items.title: Sector-Specific Analysis
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should describe how different sectors (e.g., Financials/Banks' NIM, Cyclicals, Defensive stocks) react differently to changes in the yield curve slope.

rar_items.title: Steepening/Flattening Nuances
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides additional depth by distinguishing between 'Bull' and 'Bear' steepening/flattening based on the drivers of short-term vs. long-term rates.

rar_items.title: Investment Strategy Suggestions
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response suggests practical portfolio adjustments, such as duration management or sector rotation, based on the interest rate environment.

rar_items.title: Over-Simplification Pitfall
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response presents the yield curve inversion as a 100% guaranteed predictor of immediate recession without mentioning time lags or potential false signals.

rar_items.title: Factual Inaccuracy
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response incorrectly describes the relationship between interest rates and bond prices (e.g., claiming they move in the same direction).

rar_items.title: Scientific Accuracy of Habitability Factors
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must accurately explain the key scientific reasons why Earth is habitable, including the Goldilocks zone (distance from the sun), liquid water, breathable atmosphere (oxygen/pressure), and the protective magnetic field.

rar_items.title: Clarification on Water and Air Existence
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explicitly correct the user's misconception by explaining that water and atmospheres (air) exist on other planets/moons, but in forms or compositions (e.g., ice, CO2) that are unsuitable for human life.

rar_items.title: Comparative Analysis of Other Planets
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide specific examples of why other planets in the solar system (e.g., Venus, Mars, Gas Giants) are uninhabitable, contrasting their conditions with Earth's.

rar_items.title: Educational Tone and Accessibility
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: Since the user's query is written in a curious, slightly informal style, the response should be encouraging, easy to understand, and structured logically (e.g., using bullet points or sections).

rar_items.title: Distinction Between Natural and Artificial Habitability
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response adds value by mentioning that while humans cannot live naturally on other planets, technological solutions (domes, spacesuits, terraforming) could potentially allow for artificial habitation.

rar_items.title: Mention of Exoplanets
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response mentions the possibility of Earth-like planets outside our solar system to provide a broader astronomical perspective.

rar_items.title: Scientific Hallucination
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response provides scientifically incorrect information, such as claiming other planets have breathable oxygen or that humans can currently live on Mars without equipment.

rar_items.title: Overly Technical Jargon
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response uses excessive academic jargon without explanation, making it difficult for a general user to understand the core concepts.

rar_items.title: Mathematical Accuracy
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must correctly calculate the fuel efficiency (120 / 4 = 30 mpg) and the total fuel required for 450 miles (450 / 30 = 15 gallons).

rar_items.title: Persona and Format Adherence
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must be presented in the format of friends planning a trip together (e.g., dialogue, planning memo, or itinerary), rather than a simple mathematical explanation.

rar_items.title: Budgeting Integration
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should explicitly mention calculating a 'fuel budget' (e.g., using a variable for price per gallon or providing a concrete example), as requested in the prompt.

rar_items.title: Logical Flow of Planning
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The progression from calculating fuel efficiency to total fuel needs and finally to the budget/itinerary should be logical and easy to follow.

rar_items.title: Contextual Realism
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response adds realistic travel details such as rest stops, safety margins (extra fuel), or vehicle checks to enhance the 'trip planning' atmosphere.

rar_items.title: Clarity of Final Answer
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The final answer (15 gallons) should be clearly stated or summarized so the user can find the result immediately.

rar_items.title: Calculation Error
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: Any incorrect numerical result for the fuel efficiency or the total gallons required.

rar_items.title: Format Neglect
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: Providing the answer as a standard word problem solution without the requested 'friends planning a trip' scenario.

rar_items.title: Constraint Adherence (Quantity)
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must suggest exactly or approximately 5 key technologies as requested by the user.

rar_items.title: Technical Comprehensiveness
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The suggested technologies should cover the fundamental pillars of IT employment, including programming languages, version control (Git), databases (SQL), and deployment/infrastructure (Cloud/Docker).

rar_items.title: Actionable Guidance
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: For each suggested technology, the response should provide concrete examples of what to learn (e.g., specific languages like Python/Java) or how to start (e.g., LeetCode, specific projects).

rar_items.title: Career Contextualization
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should explain why these technologies are important for employment or how they relate to different roles (Frontend, Backend, Data, etc.).

rar_items.title: Practical Roadmap/Tips
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides additional value by suggesting a learning sequence, portfolio tips, or offering to customize the list based on a specific job role.

rar_items.title: Logical Structure
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The information should be organized using clear headings, numbering, or bullet points to ensure high readability.

rar_items.title: Outdated or Irrelevant Tech
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response suggests obsolete technologies or tools that are no longer industry standard for new entries in the IT field.

rar_items.title: Vague Generalizations
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response provides overly generic advice (e.g., 'Study hard', 'Learn coding') without naming specific technologies or tools.

rar_items.title: 정답의 정확성
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: 서울과 부산의 인구 밀도 비율이 약 3.5~3.7배 범위 내로 정확하게 제시되었는가.

rar_items.title: 수치적 근거 제시
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: 단순 결과값뿐만 아니라 서울과 부산 각각의 인구 밀도(명/㎢) 수치를 근거로 제시하였는가.

rar_items.title: 계산 과정의 명확성
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: 인구와 면적을 활용한 계산식이나, 밀도 간의 나눗셈 과정을 통해 결과가 도출됨을 명시하였는가.

rar_items.title: 통계적 유연성 언급
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: 통계 연도나 집계 기준에 따라 수치가 달라질 수 있다는 점을 명시하여 정보의 신뢰성을 높였는가.

rar_items.title: 단위 표기의 정확성
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: 인구 밀도 단위(명/㎢ 또는 명/km²)를 정확하게 표기하였는가.

rar_items.title: 가독성 및 구조화
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: 불렛 포인트나 줄바꿈을 사용하여 사용자가 수치를 한눈에 파악하기 쉽게 구성하였는가.

rar_items.title: 간결성
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: 불필요한 서술 없이 질문에 핵심적인 답변과 근거를 효율적으로 전달하였는가.

rar_items.title: 잘못된 수치 제공
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: 서울이나 부산의 인구 밀도 수치를 사실과 다르게 크게 잘못 기재하였는가.

rar_items.title: 근거 없는 단답형 응답
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: 계산 근거 없이 단순히 '몇 배'라는 결과만 제시하여 검증 가능성을 없앴는가.

rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: 응답은 사용자의 핵심 요청을 직접적으로 수행한다. [smoke:f3a820]

rar_items.title: Correctness of Logic
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The code must correctly implement the logic of repeating each character of string S exactly R times in the order they appear.

rar_items.title: Input Handling
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The code must correctly handle the input format, specifically reading the number of test cases T first, followed by T pairs of R and S.

rar_items.title: Output Format
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The output must strictly follow the requirement of printing the resulting string P for each test case, typically one per line.

rar_items.title: Time and Space Complexity
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The solution should be efficient enough to handle the maximum constraints (T=1,000, S length=20, R=8) without timeout or memory issues.

rar_items.title: Code Readability and Structure
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The code should be well-structured, using appropriate variable names and clear logic that is easy for a developer to understand.

rar_items.title: Provision of Examples
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides example inputs and outputs to demonstrate that the provided code works as expected.

rar_items.title: Language Versatility
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides solutions in multiple programming languages (e.g., Python and C++) to offer more options to the user.

rar_items.title: Syntax Errors
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The provided code contains syntax errors or uses undefined variables that prevent it from running.

rar_items.title: Incorrect Input Parsing
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The code fails to handle the input correctly, such as ignoring the test case count T or failing to split R and S properly.

rar_items.title: Off-by-one Errors
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The code repeats characters R-1 or R+1 times instead of exactly R times.

rar_items.title: 정확한 명칭 제공
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: 해당 어린이들의 공식 또는 일반적인 명칭(예: 에스코트 키즈, 플레이어 에스코트, 마스코트 키즈 등)을 정확하게 언급해야 한다.

rar_items.title: 핵심 이유 설명 (상징성)
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: 페어플레이(Fair Play), 인종차별 반대, 아동 권리 보호 등 축구가 추구하는 사회적 가치와 상징적 메시지를 전달한다는 점을 설명해야 한다.

rar_items.title: 핵심 이유 설명 (지역사회 및 팬 연결)
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: 지역사회와의 유대 강화, 미래 세대(어린이)에게 꿈과 특별한 경험 제공, 팬층 확대 등의 목적을 설명해야 한다.

rar_items.title: 선발 방식 안내
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: 유스 아카데미, 스폰서 이벤트, 추첨, 자선단체 연계 등 어린이들이 어떤 경로로 선정되는지에 대한 구체적인 예시를 제공해야 한다.

rar_items.title: 역할 및 절차 설명
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: 킥오프 전 입장, 라인업 정렬, 기념 촬영 후 퇴장 등 이들이 경기장에서 수행하는 구체적인 역할과 흐름을 설명해야 한다.

rar_items.title: 유사 역할과의 구분
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: 경기 중 공을 전달하는 '볼보이/볼걸'과 '에스코트 키즈'의 차이점을 명시하여 사용자의 잠재적 혼동을 방지해야 한다.

rar_items.title: 역사적 배경 제공
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: 1990년대 유럽에서의 시작이나 FIFA의 공식 프로그램 도입 등 제도적 유래나 역사적 배경을 언급하여 답변의 깊이를 더해야 한다.

rar_items.title: 부적절한 추측 및 오정보
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: 근거 없는 개인적인 추측을 사실처럼 말하거나, 에스코트 키즈의 역할을 경기 운영의 필수적인 기술적 보조 역할로 잘못 설명하는 경우.

rar_items.title: 불친절한 말투 및 태도
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: 질문자의 궁금증을 무시하거나, 지나치게 딱딱하거나 공격적인 말투를 사용하여 사용자 경험을 해치는 경우.

rar_items.title: VK Definition and Overview
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must accurately define VK (VKontakte) as a Russian-based SNS and mention its primary user base in Russia and the CIS (Commonwealth of Independent States) region.

rar_items.title: Comparative Analysis
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must provide a clear comparison between VK and Facebook, covering key dimensions such as market reach, user demographics, and platform identity.

rar_items.title: Feature Comparison
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should compare specific features of both platforms, such as news feeds, groups/communities, messaging, and advertising tools.

rar_items.title: Super-App Characteristics
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should highlight VK's 'super-app' nature, specifically mentioning integrated services like music streaming, mini-apps, and local payment systems (e.g., VK Pay).

rar_items.title: Meta Ecosystem Synergy
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should mention Facebook's strength in its integrated ecosystem with other Meta apps like Instagram and WhatsApp.

rar_items.title: Regulatory and Political Context
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should discuss the different regulatory environments, including Russian data localization laws for VK and global privacy regulations (like GDPR) or access restrictions in Russia for Facebook.

rar_items.title: Strategic Recommendation
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides actionable advice on which platform to choose based on specific goals (e.g., targeting the Russian market vs. global expansion).

rar_items.title: Factual Accuracy
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response contains outdated or incorrect information regarding the ownership of VK or the current accessibility of Facebook in Russia.

rar_items.title: Structural Clarity
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response uses a well-organized structure (e.g., bullet points, tables, or numbered lists) to make the comparison easy to read.

rar_items.title: Target Audience Appropriateness
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The language, vocabulary, and tone must be suitable for an elementary school student, avoiding overly technical jargon without immediate, simple explanation.

rar_items.title: Use of Intuitive Analogies
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: Complex quantum concepts (e.g., superposition, entanglement) must be explained using everyday analogies such as spinning coins, magic gloves, or Lego blocks.

rar_items.title: Core Concept Coverage
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should cover key pillars of quantum mechanics, such as wave-particle duality, superposition/measurement, and entanglement, in a simplified manner.

rar_items.title: Practical Application Examples
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should connect abstract theories to real-world technology (e.g., smartphones, LED, MRI) to demonstrate why the subject is important.

rar_items.title: Structural Clarity
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The information should be organized using bullet points, numbered lists, or clear sections to prevent the child from feeling overwhelmed by a wall of text.

rar_items.title: Conceptual Summary
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: Providing a final 'one-line summary' or a 'short wrap-up' helps reinforce the main takeaway for a young learner.

rar_items.title: Encouraging Tone
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: Using a friendly, encouraging, and curious tone (e.g., 'Isn't it amazing?', 'Imagine this!') to engage the child's interest in science.

rar_items.title: Over-complication Pitfall
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: Using advanced mathematical formulas or deep theoretical physics terms (e.g., Schrödinger equation, Hilbert space) without a very simple bridge.

rar_items.title: Scientific Inaccuracy Pitfall
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: Over-simplifying to the point of creating fundamental scientific misconceptions (e.g., implying that 'consciousness' creates reality rather than 'measurement/interaction').

rar_items.title: Core Battery Type Identification
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must clearly identify LFP (Lithium Iron Phosphate) as the primary battery type preferred by companies for its balance of price, safety, and lifespan.

rar_items.title: Comparative Analysis of Chemistry
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must provide a clear comparison between LFP and NMC/NCA, specifically contrasting energy density (performance) against cost and safety.

rar_items.title: Application-Specific Recommendations
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should categorize battery preferences by use case, such as ESS (LFP), Premium EVs (NMC/NCA), and Budget EVs (LFP).

rar_items.title: Technical Trade-off Explanation
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should explain the technical reasons for preference, such as the absence of cobalt/nickel in LFP reducing cost and supply chain risk.

rar_items.title: Alternative and Next-Gen Technologies
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response mentions alternative or emerging technologies such as Sodium-ion (Na-ion), LTO, or LMFP to provide a comprehensive industry outlook.

rar_items.title: Form Factor and Packaging Trends
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response discusses the relationship between battery chemistry and form factors (Prismatic, Cylindrical, Pouch) or packaging innovations like CTP (Cell-to-Pack).

rar_items.title: Business Perspective (TCO)
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response addresses the 'corporate preference' aspect by mentioning Total Cost of Ownership (TCO), supply chain stability, or regulatory compliance.

rar_items.title: Factual Inaccuracy
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response provides incorrect technical data regarding energy density, cycle life, or material composition of the mentioned battery types.

rar_items.title: Lack of Differentiation
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response fails to distinguish between different battery types, treating them as a single category or providing a vague answer without specific chemistry names.

rar_items.title: Constraint Adherence: Title
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explicitly provide or confirm the use of the exact title '우리 나라 기업의 해외진출' as requested by the user.

rar_items.title: Resource Provision: News Sources
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must provide a comprehensive list of reliable news sites, portals, or official institutions (e.g., Naver, KOTRA, KITA, major economic dailies) where the user can find articles.

rar_items.title: Practicality: Search Keywords
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide specific, actionable search keywords or query combinations (e.g., 'Company Name + Overseas Investment') to help the user find relevant articles quickly.

rar_items.title: Guidance: Assignment Framework
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide a template or a structured guide on how to organize the found articles for a school assignment (e.g., Summary, Source, Implications).

rar_items.title: Urgency Handling: Efficiency Tips
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: Given the user's urgent deadline ('by tomorrow'), the response should include tips for rapid searching, such as using date filters or selecting a few key industries.

rar_items.title: Tone and Manner: Empathy and Sincerity
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response should maintain a supportive and sincere tone, acknowledging the user's urgency and explicitly avoiding 'jokes' as requested.

rar_items.title: Value-Add: Example Cases
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides current examples of Korean companies expanding overseas (e.g., Battery, Semiconductor, Automotive) to give the user a starting point.

rar_items.title: Pitfall: Direct Article Fabrication
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response should not fabricate fake news articles or provide outdated/broken links as if they are current news.

rar_items.title: Pitfall: Over-Automation
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response should not simply write the entire assignment for the user without providing the requested 'sites' and 'methods' for finding articles.

rar_items.title: Motif Integration: 'Flower'
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must incorporate the core motif of Kim Chun-su's 'Flower' (the act of naming, calling, and the transition from a mere object to a meaningful existence).

rar_items.title: Motif Integration: 'Foreword'
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must incorporate the core motif of Yun Dong-ju's 'Foreword' (self-reflection, shame, longing for purity, and a gaze toward the sky/stars).

rar_items.title: Thematic Exploration: Nature and Humanity
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The text must explore the relationship between nature and humans, moving beyond simple description to a philosophical or emotional inquiry into their interconnectedness.

rar_items.title: Literary Style: Poetic Prose
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The writing should employ poetic devices (metaphors, imagery, rhythmic phrasing) and maintain a lyrical tone consistent with 'poetic prose' rather than a standard essay.

rar_items.title: Emotional Depth and Resonance
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should evoke a sense of contemplation, serenity, or introspection, avoiding cliché expressions and striving for original emotional insight.

rar_items.title: Structural Cohesion
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The flow of the prose should be organic, with a natural transition between the motifs of the two poems and the final conclusion on the human-nature relationship.

rar_items.title: Vocabulary Sophistication
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The use of refined, evocative Korean vocabulary that enhances the aesthetic quality of the prose.

rar_items.title: Literal Plagiarism of Poems
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response should not simply quote long passages from the original poems; it must reinterpret the motifs into a new prose piece.

rar_items.title: Generic/Abstract Content
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response is penalized if it provides a generic discussion about the poems instead of actually writing a creative piece of poetic prose.

rar_items.title: Empathy and Emotional Resonance
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must deeply empathize with the emotional struggles of a regional migrant, such as feelings of alienation, self-consciousness, and the fatigue of self-monitoring, rather than just providing technical advice.

rar_items.title: Analysis of Communication Barriers
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response should specifically identify the causes of communication issues, including differences in intonation (prosody), vocabulary, and the nuance of honorifics/politeness levels between dialects and standard Korean.

rar_items.title: Practical Adaptation Strategies
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide actionable and realistic tips for adapting to standard Korean, such as 'code-switching' based on the situation, shadowing media, or creating a set of 'safe' standard phrases.

rar_items.title: Identity Preservation Perspective
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should frame the use of dialects not as a 'defect' to be fixed, but as a part of one's identity and a potential asset (bilingual-like capability), encouraging the user to maintain their roots.

rar_items.title: Situational Coping Mechanisms
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides specific scripts or behavioral guidelines for handling difficult social situations, such as when others mock or mimic the user's dialect.

rar_items.title: Structure and Readability
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response is well-organized using headings, bullet points, or clear sections to separate empathy, analysis, and practical advice, making it easy to digest.

rar_items.title: Judgmental or Corrective Tone
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response adopts a tone that treats dialects as 'incorrect' or 'wrong' and focuses solely on 'correcting' them to standard Korean without emotional support.

rar_items.title: Generic or Superficial Advice
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response provides overly generic advice (e.g., 'just practice more' or 'be confident') without acknowledging the specific linguistic and social nuances of the Korean dialect-standard divide.

rar_items.title: 사건 명칭의 정확성
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: 폐비 윤씨와 관련된 사화가 '갑자사화'임을 정확하게 명시해야 한다.

rar_items.title: 발생 시기의 정확성
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: 사건이 일어난 시기(1504년 또는 연산군 10년)를 정확하게 제시해야 한다.

rar_items.title: 핵심 피해 세력 식별
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: 폐비 윤씨 사건의 직접적인 관련자인 '훈구 세력(공신, 대신)'이 주된 피해자였음을 명확히 설명해야 한다.

rar_items.title: 사건 배경 설명
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: 연산군이 생모 폐비 윤씨의 폐출 및 사사 사실을 알게 되어 보복했다는 인과관계를 설명해야 한다.

rar_items.title: 피해 범위의 상세함
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: 훈구 세력 외에도 사림 세력이나 궁중 인물들이 광범위하게 피해를 입었다는 부수적 피해 사실을 함께 언급해야 한다.

rar_items.title: 역사적 용어의 적절성
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: '폐출', '사사', '연좌' 등 당시의 상황을 설명하는 적절한 역사적 용어를 사용하여 전문성을 높였는가.

rar_items.title: 가독성 및 구조화
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: 시기, 배경, 피해 세력을 구분하여 사용자가 읽기 편하게 구조화(불렛 포인트 등)하여 제공했는가.

rar_items.title: 사실 관계 왜곡
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: 갑자사화의 주된 피해 세력을 훈구가 아닌 사림으로만 단정 짓거나, 무오사화와 혼동하여 설명하는 경우.

rar_items.title: 정보 누락
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: 요청한 사항 중 '언제' 또는 '피해 세력' 중 어느 하나라도 누락하여 답변한 경우.

rar_items.title: Recommendation Count
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must recommend exactly 3 open-source projects as requested.

rar_items.title: Relevance to Reproducibility
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The recommended projects must be specifically focused on or capable of ensuring 'reproducibility' (e.g., Nix, Bazel, OCI Containers/Docker, GNU Guix).

rar_items.title: Technical Accuracy of Descriptions
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The descriptions must accurately explain the technical mechanism that enables reproducibility (e.g., content-addressable storage, hermetic builds, image digests, declarative specs).

rar_items.title: Persona Alignment
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The tone and depth of the explanation should reflect the expertise of a 'DevOps Professional Engineer', using appropriate industry terminology.

rar_items.title: Conciseness and Clarity
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: Each project description should be concise and clear, avoiding unnecessary verbosity while maintaining core technical points.

rar_items.title: Practical Application Context
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides specific use cases or tips on how to actually achieve reproducibility with the tool (e.g., mentioning flake.lock, multi-stage builds, or remote caching).

rar_items.title: Formatting and Readability
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response uses structured formatting (bullet points, numbering, or bold text) to make the information easy to scan.

rar_items.title: Generic/Irrelevant Recommendations
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: Recommending general-purpose tools that do not inherently guarantee reproducibility without significant external configuration (e.g., simple shell scripts or basic VM snapshots).

rar_items.title: Hallucinated Features
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: Attributing features to a project that it does not actually possess or misrepresenting how the tool achieves reproducibility.

rar_items.title: Dietary Classification Accuracy
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must clearly categorize dinosaurs into three main dietary groups: herbivores (초식), carnivores (육식), and omnivores (잡식).

rar_items.title: Representative Examples
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must provide accurate examples of dinosaurs for each dietary category (e.g., Triceratops/Brachiosaurus for herbivores, T-Rex for carnivores).

rar_items.title: Scientific Evidence Explanation
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should explain how scientists determine dinosaur diets, mentioning evidence such as tooth shape, coprolites (fossilized dung), or stomach contents.

rar_items.title: Botanical Detail
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: For herbivores, the response should specify the types of plants available during the Mesozoic era (e.g., ferns, cycads, conifers, or early flowering plants).

rar_items.title: Taxonomic Distinction
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides additional value by clarifying that pterosaurs (익룡) and marine reptiles (어룡/수장룡) are not technically dinosaurs.

rar_items.title: Tone and Engagement
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response uses a friendly, encouraging tone that matches the user's high level of curiosity (e.g., acknowledging the 'excitement' of the question).

rar_items.title: Factual Hallucination
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response provides scientifically incorrect information, such as claiming dinosaurs ate grass (which was not prevalent/available for most) or misclassifying a well-known species.

rar_items.title: Over-simplification
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response is too brief (e.g., only saying 'some ate plants and some ate meat') without providing the examples or evidence requested by the context of the query.

rar_items.title: Cause Analysis Accuracy
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explain why the smell occurs specifically in air purification mode (e.g., lack of condensation/cooling compared to cooling mode, filter saturation, or ionizer operation).

rar_items.title: Filter-Specific Guidance
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must distinguish between different filter types (Pre-filter, HEPA, Deodorizing/Carbon) and provide correct maintenance for each (e.g., washing pre-filters vs. replacing carbon filters).

rar_items.title: Ionizer/Plasma Mention
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should mention that ionizers or plasma functions can produce ozone or metallic smells that users may perceive as 'pungent' or 'urine-like'.

rar_items.title: Step-by-Step Troubleshooting
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide a logical sequence of actions, starting from the easiest/cheapest (cleaning filters) to the most complex (professional disassembly cleaning).

rar_items.title: Internal Drying Advice
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response must suggest using the 'Internal Dry' or 'Fan' mode after cooling/dehumidifying to prevent mold and bacterial growth.

rar_items.title: Professional Service Threshold
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should clearly define when self-care is insufficient and professional disassembly cleaning (deep cleaning) is necessary.

rar_items.title: Safety and Cautionary Warnings
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response should include safety warnings, such as disconnecting power before cleaning or avoiding certain chemicals (e.g., bleach) on the coils.

rar_items.title: Request for Specific Model
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response should offer to provide more tailored instructions if the user provides the specific model name of the air conditioner.

rar_items.title: Incorrect Cleaning Advice
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response must not suggest washing non-washable filters (like HEPA or Carbon filters) with water, as this destroys their functionality.

rar_items.title: Vague or Generic Answers
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response should not simply say 'it is dirty' without explaining the mechanism of why it happens only in air purification mode.

rar_items.title: Project Identification
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must suggest a representative open-source project for high availability (HA) on Linux servers (e.g., Pacemaker, Keepalived, etc.).

rar_items.title: Detailed Project Description
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must provide a detailed explanation of the project, including its purpose, core components, and how it achieves high availability.

rar_items.title: Analysis of Advantages
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must clearly list the advantages of the suggested project, such as stability, flexibility, or community support.

rar_items.title: Analysis of Disadvantages
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must clearly list the disadvantages or limitations, such as learning curve, operational complexity, or specific infrastructure requirements.

rar_items.title: Clarity and Conciseness
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The information should be presented in a clear, concise, and well-structured format (e.g., using bullet points or sections) as requested.

rar_items.title: Technical Accuracy
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The technical details regarding the project's architecture (e.g., the relationship between Pacemaker and Corosync) must be accurate.

rar_items.title: Contextual Use Cases
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides practical examples or scenarios where the project is most effectively applied.

rar_items.title: Comparison with Alternatives
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response mentions when this project is more or less suitable compared to other modern alternatives (e.g., Kubernetes).

rar_items.title: Irrelevant Information
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response includes excessive fluff, unrelated technical jargon, or information that does not contribute to the explanation of the HA project.

rar_items.title: Missing Required Sections
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response fails to include any one of the requested parts: project suggestion, detailed description, advantages, or disadvantages.

rar_items.title: Conceptual Accuracy
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must accurately define postmodernism as a reaction against modernism, emphasizing the rejection of universal truths (meta-narratives), rationality, and progress, while highlighting plurality, relativity, and fragmentation.

rar_items.title: Theoretical Foundation
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should mention key theorists or core concepts associated with postmodernism, such as Lyotard (meta-narratives), Derrida (deconstruction), Baudrillard (simulacra/hyperreality), or Foucault (power/knowledge).

rar_items.title: Diversity of Examples
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must provide concrete examples across multiple domains (e.g., architecture, art, literature, film, or digital culture) to illustrate how postmodern concepts are applied in practice.

rar_items.title: Connection between Theory and Example
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should not just list examples but explain *why* a specific example (e.g., Andy Warhol's pop art or Tarantino's films) is considered postmodern by linking it back to the core concepts.

rar_items.title: Balanced Perspective
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides a balanced view by discussing both the contributions (e.g., empowering minority voices) and the criticisms (e.g., excessive relativism, loss of depth) of postmodernism.

rar_items.title: Length and Structure
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should be comprehensive enough to satisfy the '1-page' request, utilizing a well-organized structure (e.g., headings, bullet points) for readability.

rar_items.title: Contemporary Relevance
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response connects postmodernism to modern digital phenomena, such as memes, AI-generated content, or social media, to show the ongoing relevance of the theory.

rar_items.title: Factual Hallucination
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response attributes theories to the wrong philosophers or provides historically inaccurate examples of postmodern works.

rar_items.title: Over-simplification
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response defines postmodernism too vaguely (e.g., simply as 'something new' or 'modern art') without explaining the philosophical shift from modernism.

rar_items.title: Cause Identification
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must correctly identify the white powder as mineral deposits (limescale/calcium/magnesium) or food residue (protein/salt) to provide the correct chemical solution.

rar_items.title: Acidic Solution Recommendation
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must suggest using acidic substances such as vinegar, citric acid, or lemon juice to dissolve the mineral deposits.

rar_items.title: Material-Specific Guidance
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide different precautions or methods based on the pot's material (e.g., Stainless Steel, Aluminum, Non-stick coating) to prevent damage.

rar_items.title: Step-by-Step Instructions
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide a clear, sequential process (e.g., adding water/vinegar -> boiling -> soaking -> scrubbing) that is easy for a beginner to follow.

rar_items.title: Prevention and Maintenance Tips
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides additional tips to prevent the recurrence of white powder, such as drying the pot immediately after washing.

rar_items.title: Alternative Solutions
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response suggests alternative household items (e.g., baking soda for protein stains, cola for emergencies) if the primary method fails.

rar_items.title: Dangerous Tool Warning
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response fails to warn the user against using sharp or metal objects to scrape the pot, which could damage the surface or coating.

rar_items.title: Chemical Mixing Danger
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response suggests mixing incompatible cleaning agents (e.g., mixing vinegar/acid with bleach) without a strong warning against it.

rar_items.title: Aluminum-Alkali Warning
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response suggests using baking soda (alkaline) on aluminum pots without warning that it can cause discoloration.

rar_items.title: Mathematical Logic Accuracy
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must correctly calculate that Chul-soo needs 50 more levels to reach level 55 and that he would normally achieve this in 50 days, which is shorter than the demon's 100-day requirement.

rar_items.title: Winning Condition Derivation
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explicitly state that the demon's goal is to prevent Chul-soo from reaching level 55 within the 100-day window (i.e., keeping him at level 54 or below until day 100).

rar_items.title: Quantitative Strategy
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide a quantitative target for the demon, such as reducing the average level-up rate to less than 0.5 levels per day or ensuring at least 51 days of no growth.

rar_items.title: Diversity of Actionable Measures
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should suggest a variety of concrete methods to hinder growth, such as blocking experience sources, using debuffs/curses, psychological warfare, or physical confinement.

rar_items.title: Strategic Coherence
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The suggested actions must logically align with the goal of 'delaying' or 'reducing growth rate' rather than just suggesting a direct fight (which would be risky before day 100).

rar_items.title: Structure and Readability
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response is well-structured using bullet points or sections (e.g., Premise -> Calculation -> Strategy -> Summary) for better clarity.

rar_items.title: Creative Problem Solving
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response suggests creative or non-obvious strategies, such as manipulating the game world's economy, social engineering, or using 'fake quests' to waste time.

rar_items.title: Calculation Error
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response incorrectly calculates the required levels (e.g., saying he needs 55 levels instead of 50) or the time required.

rar_items.title: Logical Contradiction
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response suggests the demon should fight Chul-soo immediately without considering the level difference or the 100-day power-up condition.

rar_items.title: Direct Answer and Conclusion
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must provide a clear and immediate conclusion (e.g., 'You do not have to yield the seat') at the beginning to resolve the user's dilemma quickly.

rar_items.title: Safety and Health Prioritization
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must emphasize that the user's physical safety (risk of falling, surgical recovery) takes precedence over social norms of yielding to the elderly in this specific context.

rar_items.title: Practical Communication Guide
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide concrete, polite phrasing or scripts that the user can use to explain their situation to the elderly person or other passengers.

rar_items.title: Contextual Analysis of Priority Seats
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should explain the nature of 'priority seats' (transportation vulnerable), clarifying that injured persons are also legitimate users of these seats.

rar_items.title: Alternative Solutions
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response suggests alternative ways to be helpful without yielding the seat, such as asking other passengers for help or offering small gestures of kindness.

rar_items.title: Psychological Support
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response includes empathetic language to alleviate the user's guilt or anxiety regarding the social pressure to yield.

rar_items.title: Judgmental or Pressuring Tone
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response should not pressure the user to yield the seat despite their injury or judge them for prioritizing their own health.

rar_items.title: Vague or Ambiguous Advice
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response avoids giving an ambiguous 'it depends' answer without providing a clear decision-making framework for the user's specific situation.

rar_items.title: Comparative Analysis of Nature Expression
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must clearly contrast how nature is used in both poems, specifically distinguishing between the 'ritualistic/concrete' nature in 'Azaleas' (e.g., specific locations, act of scattering flowers) and the 'symbolic/temporal' nature in 'Until the Peonies Bloom' (e.g., the cycle of blooming and falling as a measure of time).

rar_items.title: Contrast of Emotional Tones of Love
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must analyze the difference in the emotion of love, contrasting the 'self-sacrifice, resignation, and sorrow (Han)' in Kim So-wol's work with the 'longing for aesthetic perfection and the subsequent void/emptiness' in Kim Young-lang's work.

rar_items.title: Analysis of Poetic Devices and Rhythm
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should discuss the formal differences, such as the folk-song rhythm (minyo-jo) and colloquial tone of Kim So-wol versus the refined, symbolic, and sensory language of Kim Young-lang.

rar_items.title: Temporal and Spatial Framework
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should identify the spatial focus (the 'path' of parting) in 'Azaleas' and the temporal focus (the 'season' of waiting and loss) in 'Until the Peonies Bloom'.

rar_items.title: Symbolic Meaning of the Flowers
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should explain the symbolic difference between the 'Azalea' (humble, native, medium of parting) and the 'Peony' (luxurious, peak of beauty, symbol of ideal/loss).

rar_items.title: Synthesis and Conclusion
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides a high-level synthesis that connects these differences to the broader literary characteristics of the two poets (e.g., traditional folk sentiment vs. modern aestheticism).

rar_items.title: Factual Accuracy of Poem Content
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response misquotes the poems or attributes characteristics of one poem to the other poet.

rar_items.title: Lack of Comparative Structure
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response describes the two poems separately without providing a direct comparison or contrast between them.

rar_items.title: Accuracy of Base Data (War Casualties)
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must provide a reasonable estimate for Korean War deaths, typically within the range of 2 million to 4 million people.

rar_items.title: Accuracy of Base Data (Seoul Population)
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must use a current and accurate estimate for the 2023 Seoul population, approximately 9.4 million people.

rar_items.title: Mathematical Correctness
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The percentage calculation (War Deaths / Seoul Population * 100) must be mathematically accurate based on the provided base data.

rar_items.title: Clarity of Calculation Process
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should explicitly show the numbers used for the calculation (numerator and denominator) so the user can verify the result.

rar_items.title: Handling of Estimation Ranges
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: Since casualty numbers vary by source, the response should ideally provide a range of percentages or mention that the result depends on the chosen estimate.

rar_items.title: Directness of Answer
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The final estimated percentage should be clearly stated, preferably at the beginning or end of the response for quick readability.

rar_items.title: Contextual Nuance
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response adds value by mentioning that casualty figures can vary depending on whether civilians, soldiers, or both sides are included.

rar_items.title: Hallucination of Data
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response provides wildly inaccurate numbers for either the war deaths or the Seoul population (e.g., confusing millions with thousands).

rar_items.title: Calculation Error
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response provides the correct base numbers but arrives at a mathematically incorrect percentage.

rar_items.title: Perspective Alignment
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explicitly frame the explanation from a Korean person's perspective, contrasting Korean 'Ah-Ah' (Iced Americano) culture with Italian coffee traditions.

rar_items.title: Cultural Contrast Analysis
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explain the fundamental difference between the 'long, cold, and slow' drinking habit of Koreans and the 'short, hot, and fast' espresso bar culture of Italians.

rar_items.title: Technical Coffee Reasoning
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should mention specific reasons why Italians dislike the dilution of coffee, such as the loss of crema, body, and aroma caused by ice and water.

rar_items.title: Alternative Recommendations
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide specific Italian alternatives to Iced Americano (e.g., Caffè Shakerato, Caffè Freddo, Granita) to show that they don't hate cold coffee itself, but rather the Americano style.

rar_items.title: Practical Travel Tips
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides useful phrases or ordering tips for Koreans visiting Italy to get a similar drink or navigate the local cafe system.

rar_items.title: Nuanced Generalization
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should avoid over-generalizing by mentioning that younger generations or tourist areas in Italy are becoming more open to Iced Americanos.

rar_items.title: Structural Clarity
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response uses clear formatting (bullet points, bold text, or summaries) to make the comparison between the two cultures easy to read.

rar_items.title: Factual Hallucination
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response claims that Italians fundamentally hate all cold beverages or provides incorrect Italian terminology for coffee menus.

rar_items.title: Lack of Perspective
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response provides a generic explanation of Italian coffee culture without addressing the 'Korean perspective' requested in the prompt.

rar_items.title: Correctness of Core Conclusion
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must clearly state that there is no official rule or intentional design to make Group F the 'Group of Death', and that it is primarily a result of coincidence, cognitive bias, and memes.

rar_items.title: Explanation of Cognitive Bias
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response should explain psychological phenomena such as confirmation bias or availability heuristic (e.g., remembering only the strong Group Fs and forgetting others).

rar_items.title: Provision of Concrete Examples
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide specific historical examples of 'Group F' being a group of death (e.g., 2002 World Cup, Euro 2020) to illustrate why the perception exists.

rar_items.title: Counter-examples for Balance
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: To prove the lack of a pattern, the response should mention that other groups (A, B, C, D, G, etc.) have also been the 'Group of Death' in different tournaments.

rar_items.title: Addressing Non-Football Sports
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: Since the user specifically mentioned other sports like StarCraft, the response must address how the 'Group F' phenomenon applies to e-sports or other competitive formats.

rar_items.title: Technical Analysis of Draw Process
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response may explain the technicalities of seed pots, continental restrictions, or the 'last group effect' where remaining strong teams are forced into later groups during a live draw.

rar_items.title: Structure and Readability
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response should be well-structured (e.g., using bullet points or a 'Short Answer' followed by a 'Detailed Explanation') for better readability.

rar_items.title: Misleading Causality
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response must not falsely claim that there is a hidden rule, a secret conspiracy, or a mathematical certainty that makes Group F stronger than others.

rar_items.title: Over-generalization
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response should not ignore the user's specific mention of e-sports and only focus on football.

rar_items.title: Correctness of Conclusion
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must clearly state that the claim is 'wrong' (틀린 말) and that money is not 'copied' or created out of nothing.

rar_items.title: Identification of Double Counting
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explain that the error stems from 'double counting' (이중계산), specifically that A treats their own initial 10,000 won as a gain when buying the box.

rar_items.title: Zero-Sum Logic Explanation
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must logically demonstrate that the total sum of assets between A and B remains constant (Zero-sum), meaning one person's gain is exactly equal to the other's loss.

rar_items.title: Detailed Asset Tracking
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide a step-by-step breakdown of cash flow or net asset changes for both A and B to prove the conclusion.

rar_items.title: Ownership Concept Application
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should explain the concept of 'ownership' or 'equity' (지분), clarifying that A is essentially buying B's share of the box.

rar_items.title: Refutation of Infinite Loop
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response must explicitly address the 'infinite repetition' part of the prompt, explaining why repeating the process does not result in money multiplication.

rar_items.title: Logical Consistency
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The mathematical calculations provided for A and B's final balance must be internally consistent and sum to zero.

rar_items.title: Clarity and Structure
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response uses formatting (bullet points, numbered lists, or sections) to make the logical flow easy to follow.

rar_items.title: Mathematical Error
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response contains calculation errors or fails to account for the initial 10,000 won investment in the final balance.

rar_items.title: Ambiguous Conclusion
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response fails to give a definitive 'True/False' answer or provides a contradictory conclusion.

rar_items.title: Scientific Explanation of Water Reduction
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explain why water does not increase linearly (e.g., mentioning that evaporation does not double just because the number of noodles doubles, or the effect of surface area/pot size).

rar_items.title: Explanation of Viscosity and Concentration
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must mention that starch from the noodles or the combined effect of multiple soup packets increases the thickness/concentration of the broth, making a linear increase in water feel bland.

rar_items.title: Appropriate Water Amount for 3 Packs
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must provide a specific recommended water amount for 3 packs of ramen (typically around 1.2L to 1.4L) based on the logic provided.

rar_items.title: Logical Consistency in Calculation
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: If a formula or rule of thumb is provided (e.g., 'first pack 550ml + additional packs 330ml'), the calculation for 3 packs must be mathematically consistent with that rule.

rar_items.title: Practical Adjustment Tips
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide practical advice for fine-tuning the taste, such as adding hot water at the end or adjusting based on toppings (eggs, vegetables) and heat intensity.

rar_items.title: Clarity and Structure
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response uses clear formatting (bullet points, bold text, or summaries) to separate the 'reason' from the 'recommendation' for better readability.

rar_items.title: Brand Variability Mention
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response mentions that recommended amounts may vary depending on the specific ramen brand or product type.

rar_items.title: Incorrect Linear Scaling
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response incorrectly suggests that water should be increased linearly (e.g., 550ml x 3 = 1650ml) without explaining why this is usually avoided in practice.

rar_items.title: Lack of Specificity
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response gives a vague answer for the 3-pack amount (e.g., 'just a bit more than 2 packs') without providing a numerical range or estimate.

rar_items.title: Identification of the Paradox
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explicitly identify the question as the 'Omnipotence Paradox' (전능 역설) to provide proper context.

rar_items.title: Logical Consistency Analysis
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must explain that the paradox arises from a logical contradiction (e.g., the coexistence of 'unable to lift' and 'omnipotence').

rar_items.title: Definition of Omnipotence
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must discuss how the definition of 'omnipotence' (e.g., ability to do all logically possible things vs. absolute power) determines the answer.

rar_items.title: Presentation of Diverse Philosophical Perspectives
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide multiple interpretations or solutions, such as Aquinas's logical possibility, self-limitation, or conceptual invalidity.

rar_items.title: Clarity of Final Conclusion
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide a clear summary or final answer (e.g., 'The question is logically meaningless' or 'No, but it doesn't limit omnipotence').

rar_items.title: Use of Analogies
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response uses helpful analogies (e.g., 'square circle' or 'married bachelor') to illustrate the concept of logical impossibility.

rar_items.title: Academic Referencing
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response mentions specific philosophers or theologians (e.g., Aquinas, Plantinga, Mavrodes) to increase authority.

rar_items.title: Over-simplification Error
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response gives a simple 'Yes' or 'No' without explaining the logical paradox or the definition of omnipotence.

rar_items.title: Logical Fallacy in Reasoning
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response fails to distinguish between 'lack of ability' and 'logical impossibility', leading to a flawed conclusion.

rar_items.title: Country-Specific Menu Accuracy
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must clearly distinguish between common hangover remedies in the US (e.g., Diner breakfast, Breakfast Burritos, Menudo/Pho) and the UK (e.g., Full English Breakfast/Fry-up, Bacon Butty, Irn-Bru).

rar_items.title: Cultural Concept Explanation
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must answer whether they have a 'hangover cure' culture (해장) and explain that while they don't have a direct equivalent to Korean 'haejang-guk', they have similar patterns of eating to recover.

rar_items.title: Hydration and Electrolytes
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should mention the importance of water and electrolyte drinks (e.g., Gatorade, Pedialyte, Lucozade) as a primary recovery method.

rar_items.title: Hair of the Dog Mention
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should mention the 'hair of the dog' (drinking more alcohol) concept, such as Bloody Marys, while ideally noting that it is not medically recommended.

rar_items.title: Medical Safety Advice
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The response should provide a warning about avoiding acetaminophen (Tylenol/Paracetamol) due to liver strain after drinking, suggesting ibuprofen or aspirin instead.

rar_items.title: Gentle Food Alternatives
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response provides suggestions for those with sensitive stomachs, such as toast, bananas, crackers, or light soups.

rar_items.title: Structure and Readability
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response uses clear formatting (bullet points, bold text, or sections) to make the comparison between the US and UK easy to read.

rar_items.title: Hallucinated Menus
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response suggests foods that are not culturally associated with the US or UK as hangover cures (e.g., suggesting traditional Korean soups as a common local habit in the US/UK).

rar_items.title: Over-generalization
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response fails to distinguish between the US and UK, grouping them as a single 'Western' entity without mentioning specific regional differences.

rar_items.title: Quantity Compliance
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The response must recommend exactly 10 movies as requested by the user.

rar_items.title: Actor Relevance
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: All recommended movies must feature Tom Cruise as a primary cast member.

rar_items.title: Genre Accuracy
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: The recommended movies must belong to the action genre or have significant action elements.

rar_items.title: Reasoning Provision
rar_items.description: Essential Criteria: Each recommended movie must be accompanied by a specific reason for the recommendation.

rar_items.title: Reasoning Quality
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The reasons should be descriptive and highlight specific elements (e.g., stunts, plot, character, or directing) rather than generic praise.

rar_items.title: Diversity of Selection
rar_items.description: Important Criteria: The list should ideally balance different series (e.g., Mission Impossible, Top Gun) and standalone films to showcase a variety of action styles.

rar_items.title: Formatting and Readability
rar_items.description: Optional Criteria: The response uses a clear list format (numbered or bulleted) that makes it easy for the user to scan the titles and reasons.

rar_items.title: Factual Accuracy
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response contains incorrect information regarding the movie titles, release years, or the actor's involvement.

rar_items.title: Hallucination
rar_items.description: Pitfall Criteria: The response recommends movies that do not exist or movies that Tom Cruise did not appear in.
